questions.question: What are the three power supply voltages defined for a UFS device, and what are their nominal values?
questions.reference_answer: UFS defines three power supplies: (1) VCC at 2.5V nominal (range 2.4-2.7V), for memory devices; (2) VCCQ at 1.2V nominal (range 1.14-1.26V), for the memory controller and optionally the PHY; (3) VCCQ2 at 1.8V nominal (range 1.70-1.95V), for the PHY and memory controller. Defined in Section 4.2 and Table 6.4.

questions.question: What does the acronym UFS stand for, and in what type of systems is it primarily designed for use?
questions.reference_answer: UFS stands for Universal Flash Storage. It is primarily designed for mobile systems, between host processing units and mass storage memory devices. Target applications include mobile phones, UMPCs, DSCs, PMPs, MP3 players. Stated in Section 4 and 4.1.

questions.question: How many devices can be connected to a single UFS port according to the current version of the standard?
questions.reference_answer: Only one device can be connected to a UFS port. Section 4.1 states 'One device per UFS port.' Section 5.1.8 confirms only one device per port.

questions.question: Which layer of the UFS architecture uses MIPI UniPro and MIPI M-PHY?
questions.explanation: The UIC is composed of MIPI UniPro (data link) and MIPI M-PHY (physical layer), as stated in Section 4.1 and 5.1.7.

questions.question: What is the active-level polarity of the RST_n hardware reset signal?
questions.explanation: Table 6.1 defines RST_n as 'Input hardware reset signal. This is an active low signal.'

questions.question: Describe the three layers of UFS communication architecture and the protocols each layer uses.
questions.reference_answer: (1) UAP - top layer with UCS (simplified SCSI), Device Manager, Task Manager. (2) UTP - encapsulates data into UPIU frames, provides UDM_SAP, UTP_CMD_SAP, UTP_TM_SAP. (3) UIC - lowest layer with MIPI UniPro (data link) and M-PHY (physical), provides UIC_SAP and UIO_SAP. Based on SCSI SAM model. Defined in Sections 4.1, 5.1, 5.4-5.6.

questions.question: What are the valid reference clock frequencies supported by a UFS device, and which cannot be used for HS-G5?
questions.reference_answer: Four frequencies: 19.2 MHz, 26.0 MHz, 38.4 MHz, and 52.0 MHz. 19.2 MHz and 26.0 MHz cannot be used for HS-G5 and above because RJRMS values exceed allowable limits. Must use 38.4 MHz or 52.0 MHz for HS-G5+. Section 6.4, Table 6.5.

questions.question: List and briefly describe all five basic UFS device power modes.
questions.reference_answer: (1) Active - responding to commands or background ops. (2) Idle - not executing any operation. (3) UFS-Sleep - reduced power, VCC may be off, M-PHY in HIBERN8. (4) UFS-DeepSleep - lowest power, VCC off, device doesn't respond to commands. (5) UFS-PowerDown - prior to completely powering off, volatile data may be lost. Section 7.4.1.

questions.question: According to Table 7.2, which reset types enable the UFS boot process?
options: A) All reset types including LU Reset
B) Power-on, HW Reset, EndPointReset, and Host UniPro Warm Reset only
C) Only Power-on Reset and HW Reset
D) Only EndPointReset and LU Reset
questions.explanation: Table 7.2 shows boot is enabled for Power-on, HW Reset, EndPointReset, and Host UniPro Warm Reset. LU Reset has boot disabled.

questions.question: What is the minimum RST_n pulse width that a UFS device is required to detect as a valid reset?
questions.explanation: Table 7.1 specifies tRSTW minimum as 1 us. Pulses <=100 ns are filtered out.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the UDM_SAP service access point in UFS architecture?
options: A) To provide physical layer reset and power mode control
B) To transport UPIU frames between UFS host and device
C) To expose UTP layer services to Device Manager for query requests
D) To trigger UniPro stack reset
questions.explanation: Section 5.1.5 defines UDM_SAP as the SAP exposed by UTP to Device Manager for UFS device-level functions.

questions.question: During power-up ramp, what condition must be maintained between VCCQ2 and VCCQ after Ta (300 mV crossing)?
options: A) VCCQ2 shall be equal to VCCQ at all times
B) VCCQ2 should be greater than VCCQ - 200 mV
C) VCCQ2 shall be at least 300 mV higher than VCCQ
D) VCCQ2 and VCCQ must ramp simultaneously
questions.explanation: Section 7.2 states: 'After Ta is reached, VCCQ2 should be greater than VCCQ - 200 mV.'

questions.question: What is the difference between EndPointReset and Logical Unit Reset in terms of their effect on UFS device state?
questions.reference_answer: EndPointReset causes full device reset - flags, attributes, UniPro attributes reset to default; all LU queues reset; link startup re-initiated; boot enabled. LU Reset only resets the addressed LU's task queue. Does NOT affect UniPro, attributes, or enable boot. Section 7.1.3, 7.1.4, Table 7.3.

questions.question: What is the LSS signal, its two valid states, and when must its value be set?
questions.reference_answer: LSS (Link Startup Sequence) signal: LSS=0 selects LS-LSS (mandatory), LSS=1 selects HS-LSS (optional). Value must be fixed before RST_n goes high. In HS-LSS the device auto-detects REF_CLK frequency; in LS-LSS bRefClkFreq attribute specifies it. Table 6.1, Section 6.4.

questions.question: According to Table 7.3, which reset type does NOT reset the 'Power on reset Attributes and Flags'?
questions.explanation: EndPointReset does NOT reset power-on reset attributes/flags (Table 7.3 shows 'Not affected' for EndPointReset).

questions.question: What is the absolute maximum voltage on VCCQ, and what is the DC operating maximum?
questions.explanation: Table 6.12: VCCQ absolute max 1.5V. Table 6.4: DC operating max 1.26V (nominal 1.2V).

questions.question: Explain the conditions under which the reference clock may be turned off, and constraints when transitioning from HS-MODE to LS-MODE before gating.
questions.reference_answer: REF_CLK may be turned off only when both sub-links are in LS-MODE (SLEEP/PWM-BURST) or HIBERN8. When transitioning from HS-MODE, must wait bRefClkGatingWaitTime before gating. During PA_INIT, REF_CLK shall NOT be gated. Must be ON and stable before transitioning to STALL from SLEEP/HIBERN8. Section 6.4.

questions.question: What are the keyword definitions for 'Shall' and 'Should' in the UFS spec, and why distinguish them?
questions.reference_answer: 'Shall' = mandatory requirement for conformance, no deviation permitted. 'Should' = recommended but not mandatory. Critical distinction: 'shall' requirements are binding for interoperability; 'should' are best practices. Section 3.3.

questions.question: About bActiveICCLevel, which statement is correct?
options: A) Can be changed during active data transfers
B) Valid values 00h-0Fh; setting while LU queue non-empty may cause General Failure
C) Defines only VCC current; VCCQ/VCCQ2 always at maximum
questions.explanation: Valid values 00h-0Fh (16 levels). Setting when any LU queue non-empty may fail with General Failure. Section 7.4.1.1.

questions.question: How to exit UFS-DeepSleep power mode, and what mode does device transition to?
options: A) START STOP UNIT command; to Active
C) Hardware reset or power cycle; to UFS-Sleep or Active depending on bInitPowerMode
D) Hardware reset only; unconditionally to UFS-Sleep
questions.explanation: Section 7.4.1.6: 'Only way to exit is hardware reset or power cycle.' Transitions to UFS-Sleep or Active depending on bInitPowerMode.

questions.question: What PWM gear must a UFS device mandatorily support, and which PWM gear is explicitly excluded from use?
questions.reference_answer: A UFS device shall support PWM-G1 only. PWM-G0 cannot be used because it is not supported by MIPI-UniPro. Section 8.5.

questions.question: What is the Transaction Code for a COMMAND UPIU?
questions.explanation: Table 10.1: COMMAND UPIU = 00 0001b.

questions.question: What are the minimum and maximum sizes of a UPIU?
questions.reference_answer: Minimum 32 bytes (12-byte header + 20-byte transaction specific fields). Maximum 65600 bytes. Section 10.3.

questions.question: In the UPIU Basic Header, what does bit 5 of the Transaction Type byte indicate?
options: C) Direction of flow and originator
questions.explanation: Bit 5 indicates direction: 0=Initiator originated, 1=Target originated.

questions.question: Describe the role of the READY TO TRANSFER (RTT) UPIU in a write transaction. What limits the max bytes in a single RTT?
questions.reference_answer: RTT is issued by Target when ready to receive data blocks for a write command. It paces Data Out transfer. Host shall not send DATA OUT before receiving RTT. Max bytes per RTT limited by bMaxDataOutSize attribute. Section 10.2.2, 10.7.5.

questions.question: In RESPONSE UPIU, if Flags.O=1 and Residual Transfer Count=N, what does this mean?
options: A) Target transferred N fewer bytes than requested
B) Target had N more bytes available than requested
C) UTP Data Out Mismatch error of N bytes
questions.explanation: Flags.O (Overflow) = 1 means target has more data than requested.

questions.question: Explain the UniPro addressing scheme in UFS. What are the reset values of Device ID and CPort ID?
questions.reference_answer: Two levels: Network Layer Device ID (L3) and CPort ID (L4). Reset values: Host Device ID=0, Device Device ID=1. Both CPort ID reset to 0. Only one CPort per side. Section 9.5, Table 9.2.

questions.question: Which Task Management Function queries whether a specific task is present in the queue?
questions.explanation: Query Task (80h) queries a specific task by IID, LUN, Task Tag.

questions.question: What are the two Query Function field values for QUERY REQUEST UPIU?
questions.reference_answer: 01h = STANDARD READ REQUEST, 81h = STANDARD WRITE REQUEST. 40h-7Fh vendor-specific read, C0h-FFh vendor-specific write. Section 10.7.8.2, Table 10.34.

questions.question: What is the default M-RX termination in PWM-BURST vs HS-BURST state?
options: A) On in PWM-BURST, optional in HS-BURST
B) Off in PWM-BURST, on in HS-BURST
questions.explanation: Section 8.1: Off by default in PWM-BURST, on by default in HS-BURST.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Flags.T field in a DATA OUT UPIU?
questions.reference_answer: Flags.T=1 indicates retransmit data from an RTT with Flags.T=1. Only available if out-of-order sequencing enabled (bDataOrdering=01h or 03h). Section 10.7.3.1.

questions.question: In COMMAND UPIU, when both Flags.R and Flags.W are '0', what happens to Expected Data Transfer Length?
options: A) Set to max buffer size
B) Ignored because no data transfer required
C) Set to zero and DATA IN follows
questions.explanation: No data transfer when both R and W are 0; field is ignored.

questions.question: Describe the UniPro sub-layer hierarchy and which DME primitives a UFS Device is prohibited from using.
questions.reference_answer: Hierarchy: PHY (M-PHY), PHY Adapter (L1.5), Data Link (L2), Network (L3), Transport (L4), DME. Prohibited: DME_PEER_GET/SET, DME_POWERON/POWEROFF, DME_ENDPOINTRESET, DME_HIBERNATE_ENTER/EXIT, DME_POWERMODE, DME_TEST_MODE. Section 9.2, 9.7.5.

questions.question: In UPIU header, where are IID and EXT_IID encoded for a COMMAND UPIU?
options: A) IID in byte 4[7:4]; EXT_IID in byte 5[7:4]
B) IID in byte 4[3:0]; EXT_IID in byte 7[7:4]
C) IID in byte 4[7:4]; EXT_IID in byte 7[7:4]
D) IID in byte 5[7:4]; EXT_IID in byte 7[7:4]
questions.explanation: IID in bits[7:4] of byte 4. For host-to-device UPIUs, EXT_IID in bits[7:4] of byte 7.

questions.question: What is the maximum combined EHS size, how is Total EHS Length calculated, and which UPIU types support EHS?
questions.reference_answer: Max 96 bytes. Value = INTEGER((bytes+31)/32), in 32-byte units (0-3). Only COMMAND UPIU and RESPONSE UPIU. Section 10.6.2, Table 10.3.

questions.question: In DISABLE and HIBERNATE states, what is M-TX and M-RX behavior?
options: A) M-TX drives differential zero, M-RX fully terminated
B) M-TX drives High-Z, M-RX uses Dif-Z keeper
C) M-TX drives High-Z, M-RX has no termination
questions.explanation: Section 8.1: M-TX High-Z, M-RX uses Dif-Z keeper (weak differential zero).

questions.question: What happens when WRITE DESCRIPTOR OPCODE has LENGTH not equal to descriptor size?
questions.reference_answer: Operation fails, descriptor not updated, Query Response = FAILURE. Only entire descriptor can be written. Section 10.7.8.5.

questions.question: What UniPro DLL Traffic Class and Flow Control does UFS require?
options: A) TC0 required; TC1 optional; E2E FC on; CSD on
B) TC0 required; TC1 not required; CSD disabled; CSV disabled
D) Both TC0 and TC1 required
questions.explanation: TC0 required, TC1 not. CSD and CSV disabled. Section 9.7.2, 9.7.4.

questions.question: How many Sense Data bytes in a RESPONSE UPIU for SCSI, and what is Data Segment Length?
questions.reference_answer: 18 bytes fixed format (Response Code 70h). Data Segment Length = 20 bytes. Section 10.7.2.1.

questions.question: What does Toggle Flag opcode (08h) do?
questions.explanation: Negates the current Boolean value. SELECTOR shall be zero. Section 10.7.8.11.

questions.question: What is the operation code for the INQUIRY command in UFS?
questions.reference_answer: Operation code 12h. Requests info about logical units and target device. EVPD=0/PageCode=0 returns Standard INQUIRY DATA (>=36 bytes). Section 11.3.2.

questions.question: What are the two fields in the OPERATION CODE byte of a UFS CDB?
questions.reference_answer: GROUP CODE (8 groups) and COMMAND CODE (32 codes per group). Always the first byte. Section 11.1.1.

questions.question: What is the operation code for READ (10) and what does TRANSFER LENGTH=0 mean?
questions.reference_answer: Opcode 28h. Transfer length 0 means no blocks read; not an error. Section 11.3.6.

questions.question: In READ CAPACITY (10), what is returned when capacity exceeds 32 bits?
questions.reference_answer: RETURNED LOGICAL BLOCK ADDRESS = FFFF FFFFh. App client should issue READ CAPACITY (16). Section 11.3.8.2.

questions.question: Describe REPORT LUNS behavior with pending UNIT ATTENTION and SELECT REPORT values.
questions.reference_answer: REPORT LUNS succeeds even with pending UNIT ATTENTION. SELECT REPORT: 00h=accessible LUs (no W-LUNs), 01h=W-LUNs only, 02h=all. Section 11.3.12.

questions.question: What are the three-tiered error codes from REQUEST SENSE?
questions.reference_answer: 18 bytes fixed format. Three tiers: Sense Key, ASC, ASCQ. Section 11.3.17.

questions.question: What happens to LBAs after FORMAT UNIT based on provisioning type?
questions.reference_answer: Fully provisioned (00h): all LBAs mapped. Thin provisioned (02h/03h): all LBAs unmapped. Read returns zero. Section 11.3.18.

questions.question: Explain the complete FFU process using WRITE BUFFER.
questions.reference_answer: MODE=0Eh. BUFFER OFFSET 4KB aligned. Activated ONLY by HW reset or power cycle. Check bDeviceFFUStatus after. Section 11.3.28.2.1.

questions.question: In UNMAP, what are defaults without Block Limits VPD page, and what if ANCHOR=1?
questions.reference_answer: Defaults: MAX UNMAP LBA COUNT = READ CAPACITY count; MAX DESCRIPTOR COUNT = 1. ANCHOR=1: CHECK CONDITION, ILLEGAL REQUEST. Section 11.3.26.

questions.question: Compare ABORT TASK and ABORT TASK SET task management functions.
questions.reference_answer: ABORT TASK: I_T_L_Q nexus, single command. ABORT TASK SET: I_T_L nexus, all commands. Both: mode params/reservations unchanged. Section 10.9.8.

questions.question: What is the difference between Send Data-In and Receive Data-Out services?
questions.reference_answer: Send Data-In: device sends data to client (reads). Receive Data-Out: device receives data from client (writes). Section 10.9.7.

questions.question: What is the minimum logical block size for UFS devices?
questions.reference_answer: 4096 bytes. Section 11.3.8.2.

questions.question: What is the operation code for WRITE (10)?
questions.explanation: WRITE (10) = 2Ah.

questions.question: In Standard INQUIRY Response, what is the VERSION field value?
questions.explanation: VERSION=06h (SPC conformance).

questions.question: When IMMED=1 in SYNCHRONIZE CACHE (10) but device doesn't support it?
questions.explanation: CHECK CONDITION, ILLEGAL REQUEST, INVALID FIELD IN CDB.

questions.question: In WRITE (10), what does GROUP NUMBER 10000b indicate?
questions.explanation: System Data characteristics.

questions.question: What is the UPIU LUN field value for the RPMB W-LUN?
questions.explanation: RPMB W-LUN = C4h.

questions.question: Which WRITE BUFFER MODE for FFU and how to activate?
options: B) MODE=0Eh; power-on or hard reset
questions.explanation: MODE=0Eh only. Activated by power-on or hard reset.

questions.question: Which task management function returns FUNCTION SUCCEEDED when task IS present?
questions.explanation: QUERY TASK returns FUNCTION SUCCEEDED if present.

questions.question: If UNMAP BLOCK DESCRIPTOR DATA LENGTH is not multiple of 16?
options: C) Last incomplete descriptor ignored; length=0 not an error
questions.explanation: Last incomplete descriptor ignored.

questions.question: In the UFS Mode Parameter Header, what is BLOCK DESCRIPTOR LENGTH always set to?
questions.reference_answer: Always 0000h because UFS doesn't implement Block Descriptors. Section 11.4.1.3.

questions.question: What are the three UFS-specific mode pages and their Page Codes?
questions.reference_answer: CONTROL (0Ah), READ-WRITE ERROR RECOVERY (01h), CACHING (08h). Table 11.66.

questions.question: What is the initial RPMB Write Counter value and what happens at max?
questions.reference_answer: Initial 00000000h. At max FFFFFFFFh, stops incrementing. Cannot be reset. Section 12.4.3.1.

questions.question: What is the RPMB Authentication Key size and type?
questions.reference_answer: 32 bytes. Write once, not erasable or readable. Section 12.4.3.1.

questions.question: Minimum RPMB W-LUN size?
questions.explanation: Multiple of 128 KB, minimum 128 KB. Section 12.4.2.

questions.question: Default WCE value in Caching Mode Page?
questions.explanation: Default WCE=1b (enabled).

questions.question: RPMB result code for key not yet programmed?
questions.explanation: 0007h = Authentication Key not yet programmed.

questions.question: Explain the three secure removal methods in UFS.
questions.reference_answer: (1) 00h: erase (default). (2) 01h: overwrite then erase. (3) 02h: overwrite, complement, random. (4) 03h: vendor-defined. Section 12.2.3.6.

questions.question: Describe the Purge Operation state machine.
questions.reference_answer: Two states: Idle (status 00h,02h,03h,04h,05h) and In Progress (01h). Transitions by fPurgeEnable flag. Section 12.2.3.3.

questions.question: Difference between Erase and Discard in UFS?
questions.reference_answer: Erase (type=03h, TPRZ=1): data unrecoverable, READ returns zero. Discard (02h, TPRZ=0): not guaranteed, READ returns any data. Section 12.2.2.2, 12.2.2.3.

questions.question: What sequential checks does RPMB Authenticated Data Write perform?
questions.reference_answer: (1) Counter expired? (2) Address valid? (3) Address+BlockCount in range? (4) BlockCount<=bRPMB_ReadWriteSize? (5) MAC check. (6) Counter match. (7) Write if pass. Section 12.4.7.3.3.

questions.question: Key differences between Normal and Advanced RPMB Messages?
questions.reference_answer: Normal: 512-byte frames via SECURITY PROTOCOL. Advanced: 60-byte EHS in COMMAND/RESPONSE UPIU, 4KB data in DATA UPIUs. Section 12.4.4.1, 12.4.5.1.

questions.question: fPurgeEnable set but queues NOT empty, what is bPurgeStatus?
questions.explanation: bPurgeStatus=04h (failed due to queue not empty).

questions.question: SECURITY PROTOCOL field value for JEDEC UFS?
questions.explanation: ECh for JEDEC UFS.

questions.question: RPMB Result Bit 7 indicates?
questions.explanation: Bit 7 = write counter expired.

questions.question: Mandatory changeable fields in Caching mode page?
questions.explanation: WCE and RCD only.

questions.question: What is RPMB Purge and why use judiciously?
questions.reference_answer: Makes overwritten RPMB copies unrecoverable. Mandatory in UFS4.0. Impacts RPMB LU life. Other ops may be rejected during purge. Section 12.2.2.4.1.

questions.question: MAC calculation differences between Normal and Advanced RPMB?
questions.reference_answer: Both HMAC SHA-256. Normal: bytes [228:511] of each frame. Advanced: first DATA IN/OUT data, then Meta Info bytes [0:27], then four 00h bytes. Section 12.4.3.2, 12.4.3.3.

questions.question: Advanced RPMB Authenticated Data Read is initiated by which command?
options: A) Both use SECURITY PROTOCOL OUT
B) Advanced: SECURITY PROTOCOL IN; Normal: SECURITY PROTOCOL OUT
C) Advanced: SECURITY PROTOCOL OUT; Normal: SECURITY PROTOCOL IN
D) Both use SECURITY PROTOCOL IN
questions.explanation: Advanced uses SECURITY PROTOCOL IN. Normal uses OUT first then IN.

questions.question: SPF bit=1 means what format and key difference?
options: B) Subpage; adds SUBPAGE CODE, PAGE LENGTH 2 bytes
questions.explanation: Subpage format: adds SUBPAGE CODE byte, PAGE LENGTH becomes 2 bytes.

questions.question: What is bBootLunEn attribute and its valid values?
questions.reference_answer: Controls active Boot LU. 00h=disabled, 01h=Boot LU A, 02h=Boot LU B. Section 13.1.2.

questions.question: Describe the fDeviceInit flag role during initialization.
questions.reference_answer: Set-only flag (IDN 01h). Host sets to 01h after boot download. Device resets to 00h when done. Host polls until 00h. Section 13.1.3.3.

questions.question: Boot W-LUN value and UPIU LUN field value?
questions.reference_answer: W-LUN=30h, LUN field=B0h. Section 13.1.2, 10.8.5.

questions.question: Explain permanent-bootable configuration (bBootEnable=02h).
questions.reference_answer: Changing bBootLunEn from 01h/02h to 00h is blocked (General failure FFh). Switching 01h<->02h allowed. Section 13.1.2.

questions.question: Describe the three phases of UFS initialization and boot.
questions.reference_answer: (1) Partial Init: M-PHY->HIBERN8, UniPro boot, NOP OUT/IN exchange. (2) Boot Transfer: TEST UNIT READY then READ to Boot W-LU. (3) Init Completion: set/poll fDeviceInit. Section 13.1.3.

questions.question: What are the four memory types for UFS logical units?
questions.reference_answer: Default, System Code (rarely updated), Non-Persistent (temp/swap), Enhanced Memory (vendor-specific). Section 13.2.2.

questions.question: What is Background Operations mode and how is urgency signaled?
questions.reference_answer: Device flash management during idle. Urgency via URGENT_BKOPS in wExceptionEventStatus. bBackgroundOpStatus: 00h-03h. fBackgroundOpsEn default=1. Section 13.4.4.

questions.question: Explain the PSA flow from initiation to 'Soldered' state.
questions.reference_answer: Set bPSAState: 00h(Off)->01h(Pre-soldering)->write data->02h(Loading Complete)->solder->power-up->first WRITE triggers 03h(Soldered, permanent). Section 13.6.2.

questions.question: Describe the three Data Out Transfer Rules for RTT.
questions.reference_answer: Rule 1: One DATA OUT per RTT. Rule 2: Outstanding RTTs <= bMaxNumOfRTT <= bDeviceRTTCap. Rule 3: DATA OUT order must match RTT order. Section 10.7.13.

questions.question: What UFS descriptor types exist and how does Configuration Descriptor locking work?
questions.reference_answer: Types: DEVICE(00h), CONFIGURATION(01h), UNIT(02h), INTERCONNECT(04h), STRING(05h), GEOMETRY(07h), POWER(08h), DEVICE HEALTH(09h). Config locked by bConfigDescrLock=01h (permanent). bConfDescContinue controls multi-step config. Section 14.1.

questions.question: Conditions before setting fPurgeEnable=1 and behavior after purge?
questions.reference_answer: All LU queues empty, bPurgeStatus=00h. After completion fPurgeEnable auto-clears. Host can also clear to interrupt. Table 14.26, 14.28.

questions.question: What is bActiveICCLevel default and what does it control?
questions.reference_answer: Controls max current in Active Mode. Range 00h-0Fh. Default from bInitActiveICCLevel in Device Descriptor. Table 14.28.

questions.question: RPMB W-LUN value and UPIU LUN field?
questions.explanation: RPMB: W-LUN=44h, LUN=C4h.

questions.question: Default fBackgroundOpsEn value?
questions.explanation: Default=1, background ops permitted.

questions.question: Change bBootLunEn 01h->00h when bBootEnable=02h?
options: C) Query fails, General failure FFh
questions.explanation: bBootEnable=02h prevents disabling boot.

questions.question: bHighPriorityLUN value for all LUs equal priority?
questions.explanation: 7Fh = all equal priority (default).

questions.question: bOutOfOrderDataEn=02h effect?
questions.explanation: 02h: DATA IN OoO enabled, DATA OUT disabled.

questions.question: Set bMaxNumOfRTT > bDeviceRTTCap?
questions.explanation: Not changed, Query Response = Invalid VALUE.

questions.question: When does device set bPSAState to 'Soldered' (03h)?
options: A) When host sets Loading Complete
C) First WRITE after power-up with Loading Complete
questions.explanation: Device sets 03h during first WRITE after power-up with bPSAState=02h.

questions.question: Which LU types excluded from Dynamic Device Capacity?
options: A) Normal and System Code LUs
B) RPMB W-LU, power-on WP, permanent WP, boot LUs
questions.explanation: RPMB, power-on WP, permanent WP, and boot LUs excluded.
